sections.paragraph:
ἀκρόβυστος, ου, ὁ (not LXX, but used by other translators of the OT; Just., D. 19, 3 οὐκ ἂν ἀ. ὁ θεὸς ἔπλασε τὸν Ἀδάμ; Mel., P. 76, 555 [Ch., so also Perler]; 92, 691) an uncircumcised pers. IPhld 6:1 (non-Judean Christians).

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

OT OT = Old Testament

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

pers. pers. = person(s)

IPhld IPhld = Ignatius to the Philadelphians—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 39
